addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Heat: rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub (₣Bb) [racing]
Paddock: .claude/jjm/jjp_uBlb.md

Next: rbrv-env-bash-sweep (₢BbAAE) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
  application of ACG's magic-string discipline.
  
  ## Goal
  Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
  path-construction sites onto it.
  
  ## Head fork (settle at mount)
  Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
  minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
  — sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
  — so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
  anchoring".
  
  ## Gate
  After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
  overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
  Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
  tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
  merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.
  
  ## Locked
  - Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
    RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
  - Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
    --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
    comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).
  
  ## Done
  Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
  untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
  tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BbAAE or mount Bb
Recent-work:
Commit     Identity  Subject                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
─────────  ────────  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
096084172  ₣Bb       Harden PCG's ASCII discipline from preference to prohibition: while PCr_101 (the charset-unsafe render transport) remains open, .puml diagram text MUST be plain ASCII and no committed PlantUML artifact (.puml/.svg) may carry the mojibake signature — a defect, not a style choice; deliberate multibyte may return only once PCr_101 is retired. Replaces the prior softer 'prefer ASCII / multibyte ok if it earns its place' wording. Scoped to PlantUML render artifacts, explicitly not a blanket repo-wide mandate (preserved historical artifacts with original corruption are out of scope).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
d663bde9f  ₣Bb       Rehome the HCG provenance archive into Memos as a self-contained memo dir (Variant-A precedent, beside the apck/cloudbuild memo dirs): the two-model (ChatGPT-5.0 / Claude Opus 4.1) authoring bake-off that produced the guide now at Tools/buk/vov_veiled/HCG-HandbookCurationGuide.md. Seven source files imported byte-for-byte from cnmp lenses/bpu-PCG-workups/ (cksum-verified, mojibake preserved as historical record) plus a framing memo.md that captures the 'Digital Mind as primary author/steward' pattern. The matching removal from cnmp is committed separately in that repo, completing the cnmp PCG exit.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
53279a062  ₣Bb       Re-render the four federation-tier diagram SVGs from the ASCII-ified sources (pairs with d56d2ef4): the pluml fixture regenerated diagrams/*.svg — em-dash mojibake gone, titles render clean ASCII hyphens (e.g. 'Federation Tier - Operator Login'), 0 corrupt entity sequences, fixture 6/6 green. Size limit raised to 200KB for this commit: the bulk is legitimate machine-generated diagram XML (verbose by nature), operator-confirmed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
19ee74373  ₣Bb       ASCII-ify the four federation-tier diagram sources: replace 16 typographic dashes (15 em U+2014, 1 en U+2013) with ASCII hyphens, sidestepping the PlantUML-server POST charset corruption (PUr_101) at the source per PUG's prefer-plain-ASCII discipline. Enables a clean-tree pluml fixture re-render to autofix the committed SVGs.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
9d61d20c3  ₣Bb       Seed PUG (PlantUml Guide): diagram source + render discipline framed as Palisade conduct at the PlantUML render membrane. Founded on PUr_101 — the pluml server's raw-text POST decodes the request body as ISO-8859-1 absent a charset, freezing UTF-8 em dashes into â€ mojibake in the four federation-tier README diagrams; robust membrane is PlantUML's deflate-encoded URL transport (charset-independent by construction), with a charset-header POST demoted to unverified fallback. Diagnosis confirmed against source hexdump + rendered-SVG entities + independent upstream forum repro. Registered PUG in the RBK acronym map beside CBG.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
67b23e4a0  ₢BbAAB    Stand up the conformance fast-tier theurge fixture (rbtdrn) — the data-driven vocabulary-eviction engine. Identifier-boundary-aware matcher over Tools/ and tt/: {kill_stem, keep_contexts} rows with Identifier and PathPrefix keep-axes, pure-corpse = empty keeps, basename/sprue awareness. Ships with ZERO production rows and four hermetic in-memory self-test cases (catch bare token, respect kept-identifier, respect path-prefix, catch filename sprue, pure-corpse no-false-positive) plus a live-scan case that engages the Tools/+tt/ walker the moment a row is added. Registered in manifest (empty colophons), lib.rs, RBTDRC_FIXTURES, and all six fast-base suites (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish). ACG names the concrete home (rbtdrn_conformance.rs) as THE evicted-term home, mirroring BUK's bug_require_clean_tree; rule 2 de-duplicated to point at it.  
6e2dcba87  ₢BbAAA    Author ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) v1 as the veiled, proprietary move-catalog — source-side complement to MCM, sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. Carries the spine (reference the home), the three-homes frame, v1 posture, the move discipline (litmus + triage/checker-proves-itself/name-identity rules), and three seed move-types (ACGm_101 magic-string->constant mutate-now; ACGm_102 name-identity detect-only; ACGm_103 typed-param lookahead detect-only). Register the mint in the CMK acronym map and the CLAUDE.md Load-Bearing forms registry; wire ACG back-refs from BCG and RCG.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
File-touch bitmap (x = pace commit touched file):

  1 A acg-v1-author-allocation-guide
  2 B vocab-eviction-mechanism

AB
xx ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md
·x lib.rs, rbtdrc_crucible.rs, rbtdrm_manifest.rs, rbtdrn_conformance.rs
x· BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md, CLAUDE.md, RCG-RustCodingGuide.md, claude-cmk-core.md

Commit swim lanes (x = commit affiliated with pace):

  1 A acg-v1-author-allocation-guide
  2 B vocab-eviction-mechanism

content.text: Let me read the paddock and docket from the gazette.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene, feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP feature
6	finalization. Each pace carries a targeted blast radius that merges trivially
7	into final feature work — no cross-cutting refactors. A seed, not a tree: scope
8	stays tight to the source/spec allocation family (magic-string -> constant
9	sweeps, decision-detail hoisting, source-documentation standards). Resist
10	becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.
11	
12	## Governing idea
13	
14	The ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) spine: don't recreate inline what has a named
15	home; reference the home. Values -> constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds
16	the named homes; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather than
17	recreate, and where prose with no spec-home actually goes.
18	
19	Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec, edit-time -> source comment
20	(operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary),
21	execution-time -> runtime announcement. Most conceptual comments are temporal
22	misallocation — design-time or execution-time knowledge dumped into the
23	edit-time medium, where it rots for lack of a forcing function.
24	
25	## Cinched
26	
27	- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
28	  authoring/allocation, barely touches asciidoc; that is MCM's domain).
29	  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. Not released at
30	  MVP.
31	- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practices, state the spine. Do NOT mandate
32	  universals ("every verb must announce") — those are candidates, confirmed
33	  pace-by-pace. No qualify-enforcement in v1.
34	- Two source-doc forms are distinct and both blessed: contract header (bounded
35	  comment, edit-time) vs intent announcement (runtime printout, execution-time).
36	  Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-param-shows-options practice, live
37	  in rbfc_require_vessel_sigil) is blessed alongside happy-path announcement.
38	
39	## Move discipline
40	
41	Every cleanup pace obeys one litmus: mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply
42	caught; everywhere else the verb is detect-and-report, never repair. Code-side
43	moves (the value resolves through a named constant; shellcheck and the fast suite
44	verify) may mutate now; spec- and document-side moves are detect-and-report until
45	the lexer/linter exists.
46	
47	Three rules ride that litmus:
48	
49	- Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought: exclude the false-positive
50	  class, declare the authoritative side before any alignment, and gate scope to
51	  settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still moving.
52	- A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is trusted;
53	  the eviction/conformance engine is the canonical home for such checks.
54	- Concept linkage is name-identity — the implementing symbol is the link (a
55	  function name equals its quoin's display-text; a tabtarget filename is a sprue;
56	  a scoped method is an inlay). A source-to-spec comment is the exception,
57	  reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
58	
59	## Sequencing & lanes
60	
61	The ACG guide and the eviction mechanism are heat-internal and buildable now, no
62	cross-heat dependency. The vocabulary work splits by who owns the terms:
63	
64	- Eviction (removing superseded terms) is gated on ₣BH — the lode-capture heat
65	  curates the KILL/KEEP list and retires the terms; the mechanism pace builds the
66	  standing theurge engine, and its rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
67	  cutovers. ACG names that engine as the canonical home for evicted-term
68	  assertions (ACG eating its own dog food — a named home, referenced not
69	  recreated).
70	- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
71	  eviction and never removes terms. The slated noun clusters are BH/BZ-safe in
72	  their terms but file-gated on ₣BH, since their descriptions share rbz_zipper.sh
73	  and the doc_briefs ₣BH is rewriting.
74	
75	Deliberately not slated until their owning heats land: reliquary / Lode / Hallmark
76	vocabulary (₣BH's lane), Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary (₣BZ's lane,
77	citizen-model), and verb clusters (₣BH curates its own verb descriptions during
78	cutover). Slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would only
79	re-create the drift this heat exists to kill.
80	
81	## Done looks like
82	
83	ACG v1 exists and is reachable from BCG/RCG; the rbrv.env literal has a single
84	named home in both bash and rust as the first worked application of the
85	magic-string discipline.
86	
87	# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAE
88	
89	## Character
90	Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
91	application of ACG's magic-string discipline.
92	
93	## Goal
94	Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
95	path-construction sites onto it.
96	
97	## Head fork (settle at mount)
98	Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
99	minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
100	— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
101	— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
102	anchoring".
103	
104	## Gate
105	After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
106	overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
107	Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
108	tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
109	merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.
110	
111	## Locked
112	- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
113	  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
114	- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
115	  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
116	  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).
117	
118	## Done
119	Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
120	untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
121	tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.
122

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene, feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP feature
finalization. Each pace carries a targeted blast radius that merges trivially
into final feature work — no cross-cutting refactors. A seed, not a tree: scope
stays tight to the source/spec allocation family (magic-string -> constant
sweeps, decision-detail hoisting, source-documentation standards). Resist
becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The ACG (Allocation Coding Guide) spine: don't recreate inline what has a named
home; reference the home. Values -> constants; concepts -> quoin-refs. MCM builds
the named homes; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather than
recreate, and where prose with no spec-home actually goes.

Three homes by when-read: design-time -> spec, edit-time -> source comment
(operational mechanics only — language idiom + Pale/foreign-boundary),
execution-time -> runtime announcement. Most conceptual comments are temporal
misallocation — design-time or execution-time knowledge dumped into the
edit-time medium, where it rots for lack of a forcing function.

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation, barely touches asciidoc; that is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. Not released at
  MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practices, state the spine. Do NOT mandate
  universals ("every verb must announce") — those are candidates, confirmed
  pace-by-pace. No qualify-enforcement in v1.
- Two source-doc forms are distinct and both blessed: contract header (bounded
  comment, edit-time) vs intent announcement (runtime printout, execution-time).
  Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-param-shows-options practice, live
  in rbfc_require_vessel_sigil) is blessed alongside happy-path announcement.

## Move discipline

Every cleanup pace obeys one litmus: mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply
caught; everywhere else the verb is detect-and-report, never repair. Code-side
moves (the value resolves through a named constant; shellcheck and the fast suite
verify) may mutate now; spec- and document-side moves are detect-and-report until
the lexer/linter exists.

Three rules ride that litmus:

- Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought: exclude the false-positive
  class, declare the authoritative side before any alignment, and gate scope to
  settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still moving.
- A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is trusted;
  the eviction/conformance engine is the canonical home for such checks.
- Concept linkage is name-identity — the implementing symbol is the link (a
  function name equals its quoin's display-text; a tabtarget filename is a sprue;
  a scoped method is an inlay). A source-to-spec comment is the exception,
  reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## Sequencing & lanes

The ACG guide and the eviction mechanism are heat-internal and buildable now, no
cross-heat dependency. The vocabulary work splits by who owns the terms:

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) is gated on ₣BH — the lode-capture heat
  curates the KILL/KEEP list and retires the terms; the mechanism pace builds the
  standing theurge engine, and its rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers. ACG names that engine as the canonical home for evicted-term
  assertions (ACG eating its own dog food — a named home, referenced not
  recreated).
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms. The slated noun clusters are BH/BZ-safe in
  their terms but file-gated on ₣BH, since their descriptions share rbz_zipper.sh
  and the doc_briefs ₣BH is rewriting.

Deliberately not slated until their owning heats land: reliquary / Lode / Hallmark
vocabulary (₣BH's lane), Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary (₣BZ's lane,
citizen-model), and verb clusters (₣BH curates its own verb descriptions during
cutover). Slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would only
re-create the drift this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG v1 exists and is reachable from BCG/RCG; the rbrv.env literal has a single
named home in both bash and rust as the first worked application of the
magic-string discipline.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAE

## Character
Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
anchoring".

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.

## Locked
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

content.text:
Mounted **₣Bb** (rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub). Next pace is **₢BbAAE** (rbrv-env-bash-sweep) [rough].

**Goal in one sentence:** give the `rbrv.env` filename literal a single named home in bash and sweep the path-construction sites onto it — the first worked application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

**But there's a gate problem worth stopping on.** The docket's own `## Gate` says this pace runs *after* ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands, because BH is actively rewriting the same path-construction surface (inscribe cutover repoints yoke/Director rbrv.env handling) and its terminal vocab scrub greps the whole tree last — landing this before BH's tail guarantees a merge conflict. The muster shows **₣BH still racing at 10/19**. The gate is not satisfied.

So the real decision is yours before any code moves:

- **Hold** — ₢BbAAE stays parked until BH lands (the docket's stated intent). Recommended.
- **Proceed anyway** — if you have reason to believe BH's remaining 9 paces won't touch the rbrv.env path sites, I can settle the head fork and run the sweep now.
- **Mount a different ₣Bb pace** — but ₢BbAAE is the next actionable one; the rest sit behind it.

Heat context: rbk-10 is pre-MVP source/spec hygiene, fed one pace at a time alongside MVP work. Two paces done (ACG v1 authored, vocab-eviction mechanism stood up). The paddock is explicit that the vocabulary work is sequenced behind ₣BH and ₣BZ — this sweep is the same gate.

How do you want to play it?
